label.text: (Task 1.1)
Increase the Diffraction Slit Size (increasing the uncertainty in position
), and observe how the diffraction pattern narrows on the photoreceptor wall (decreasing the uncertainty in momentum
(Task 1.2)
Decrease the Diffraction Slit Size (decreasing the uncertainty in position
), and observe how the diffraction pattern spreads out on the photoreceptor wall (increasing the uncertainty in momentum
(Task 1.3)
From equations (1) and (4), we wrote
. This showed that an increase in wavelength is met by an increase in the uncertainty in position to maintain equality (if
is constant). Observe how increasing the wavelength spreads out the diffraction pattern on the photoreceptor wall.
(Task 1.4)
Similarly, a decrease in wavelength is met by a decrease in the uncertainty in position. Observe how decreasing the wavelength narrows the diffraction pattern on the photoreceptor wall.
(Task 2.1)
Change the Simulation mode to treat light as particles by clicking
Show Particle Simulation
(Task 2.2)
Convert the photoreceptor wall to show expected particle distributions by clicking on
Light Wave Gradient
. Observe how the peaks and valleys of the expected particle distributions matches the light wave gradient.
(Task 2.3)
Open the Particle Simulation Settings and start a simulation of light particles passing through the slit. The location of each particle hitting the wall is marked by a yellow tick.
(Task 2.4)

Observe how the particles follow wave's anti-nodes (areas of high intensity) and avoids the node (areas of low intensity), resulting in the same distribution on the wall as the expected distribution and the light gradient.
(Task 2.5)
Modify the Diffraction Slit Size to change the probability distribution.
(Task 2.6)
Start a second particle simulation to observe how the particle distribution changed.
